rulings.Case Title: JEFFREY KWONG VS. JOHN DENNY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petitioner Jeffrey Kwong'S Motion For Order To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories (Set No. 1) And To Special Interrogatories (Set No. 1) And For Monetary Sanctions Of $360.40 On The Respondent City And County Of San Francisco, Or Attorney Margaret W. Baumgartner, State Bar #151762, Or Both For Failure To Respond To Authorized Methods Of Discovery And For Misuse Of The Discovery Process Under Code Of Civil Procedure Sections 2030.290 And 2023.010
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, October 19, 2018, Line 1, Petitioner Jeffrey Kwong'S Motion For Order To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories (Set No. 1) And To Special Interrogatories (Set No. 1) And For Monetary Sanctions Of $360.40 On The Respondent City And County Of San Francisco, Or Attorney Margaret W. Baumgartner, State Bar #151762, Or Both For Failure To Respond To Authorized Methods Of Discovery And For Misuse Of The Discovery Process Under Code Of Civil Procedure Sections 2030.290 And 2023.010 Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: DENIED, SEE BELOW. (SEE RULING ON THIRD MOTION) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepar85@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JEFFREY KWONG VS. JOHN DENNY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Order To Show Cause Why The Court Should Not Enter A Final Judgment Incorporating The Courts Initial Order Relating To The Petition Of Writ Of Mandamus And Dismissing Plaintiff'S Casue Of Action For Injunctive Relief.
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, October 19, 2018, Line 1, Order To Show Cause Why The Court Should Not Enter A Final Judgment Incorporating The Courts Initial Order Relating To The Petition Of Writ Of Mandamus And Dismissing Plaintiff'S Casue Of Action For Injunctive Relief. Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Neither party filed any papers in response to this order to show cause. The court will sign an order of final judgment affirming its prior ruling on plaintiff's mandamus causes of action and dismissing the causes of action for injunctive relief and declaratory judgment. The defendants declaration that they intends to take no further action in this matter makes any injunctive relief unnecessary and eliminates any controversy between plaintiff and defendant; thus, any declaratory relief would constitute an advisory opinion. Counsel for the defendants should prepare an appropriate order of final judgment, submit to plaintiff and deliver a copy to the court for signing at the hearing on October 19, 2018. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepar85@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT ROBINSON ET AL VS. UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, October 19, 2018, Line 1, DEFENDANT UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC., RASIER, LLC, RASIER-CA, LLC's Motion For Protective Order In light of plaintiff Medina's 1,200 discovery requests in this motor-vehicle collision case, the Uber defendants move for a protective order. Uber's counsel's declaration reveals the absence of a good faith effort to resolve the matter. Thus, lead counsel for Uber and Medina are to meet in-person, face-to-face for a minimum of 90 minutes. The Court expects a significant reduction in the number of discovery requests. On or before October 31, 2018, a joint written progress report is to be submitted to this Court. In the interim, all discovery in this litigation is STAYED. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Uber is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/rbu

rulings.Case Title: RODOLFO ERGUETA ET AL VS. ADAM J. JACOBS DC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion Of Defendant, Adam J. Jacobs, D.C. To Compel Plaintiff'S Attorney, Cutter Law, P.C. And Attorney John Parker, Jr. To Pay The Court Reporter Fee For Non-Appearance Of Plaintiff, Heather Marshall, At Her Noticed Deposition Of April 10, 2018 And For Monetary Sanctions Against Cutter Law, P.C. And John Parker, Jr.
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, October 19, 2018, Line 2 DEFENDANT ADAM JACOBS Motion Of Defendant, Adam J. Jacobs, D.C. To Compel Plaintiff'S Attorney, Cutter Law, P.C. And Attorney John Parker, Jr. To Pay The Court Reporter Fee For Non-Appearance Of Plaintiff, Heather Marshall, At Her Noticed Deposition Of April 10, 2018 And For Monetary Sanctions Against Cutter Law, P.C. And John Parker, Jr. Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant Adam J. Jacobs' motion to compel plaintiff's attorney to pay the court reporter fee is denied. Defendant was aware well in advance of the noticed deposition that plaintiff did not intend to appear on the date noticed by defendant. There was no reason to incur this expense under those circumstances. Additionally, there was no showing that alternate dates were discussed/ if defendant has been unable to negotiate a new date it could have than filed a motion to compel. No monetary sanctions will be imposed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepar85@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

Plaintiff within fifteen (15) days from the date of this order will provide full and complete responses without objection to Cal Inc's document requests. Monetary sanctions is the amount of $420. rulings.Case Title: TROY KING VS. BOYS AND GIRLS CLUBS OF SAN FRANCISCO, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, October 19, 2018, Line 3, DEFENDANT BOYS & GIRLS CLUBS OF SAN FRANCISCO's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT Defendant's demurrers to the first amended complaint are SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. The CCP §340 one-year statute of limitations applies to the cause of action for libel per se and the one pleaded communication within the year cannot be construed as libelous. Also, publication of that one communication to a third person is not alleged. The cause of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress fails to plead facts of extreme and outrageous conduct. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/rbu

rulings.Case Title: FEARGAL MAC CONULADH VS. ATARI S.A.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Quash Service Of The Summons
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, October 19, 2018, Line 4, DEFENDANT ATARI S.A.'s Motion To Quash Service Of The Summons Defendant Atari S.A. (1) moves to quash service of summons for lack of personal jurisdiction (CCP §418.10(a)(1)) and (2) demurs "on the basis of other pending actions" (CCP §430.10(c)). The motion to quash is DENIED and the demurrer OVERRULED. CCP §418.10(a)(1). Plaintiff's declaration evidences that California was the site of "[a]ll of the design and engineering work" for the Atari project that is at the litigation's core. Further, plaintiff declares he "spent considerable time living in California and directing the project." This suffices to confer California jurisdiction. In contrast, defendant's motion papers proffer no evidence, just attorney argument. CCP §430.10(c). This kind of special demurrer lies only where another action is also pending in California; that is not so here. (See Weil & Brown, California Practice Guide: Civil Procedure Before Trial (TRG 2016) §7:75, citing Leadford v. Leadford (1992) 6 Cal.App.4th 571, 575.) I note reference in the courtesy copies lodged with the Court to a "motion to dismiss" under CCP §410.30(a). The court clerk's office indicates that this motion was not filed, so I offer no opinion on it. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/rbu
